hook.text: Over one billion people worldwide live in informal settlements—roughly one in eight humans.

buildup.text: Slums grow where housing markets fail—when cities can't build fast enough for newcomers.

discovery.text: Many slums have vibrant economies, schools, and community networks despite lacking basic services.

twist.text: Bulldozing slums rarely works. The people just rebuild nearby. Upgrading them does.

climax.text: Medellín transformed its slums with cable cars, libraries, and parks—becoming a global model.

punchline.text: The answer isn't demolition. It's investment.

quiz.question: What approach has proven more effective than demolishing slums?
options: Upgrading them with infrastructure and services
